keyFindings: Condition C (Pre-seeded patterns, no real-time STM) achieved the highest mean food eaten (4.6), a 38.0% improvement over the no-memory baseline (A = 3.3333).
Full dual memory (D) vs baseline (A) food eaten: Δ=16.0%, Cohen's d=0.2703 (small effect). Dual memory is providing a measurable performance benefit.
Condition D LTM usage: 0.0% of actions sourced from LTM recall. LTM is almost never recalled — confidence threshold or trial length may need adjustment.

Pre-seeded LTM (C = 4.6) vs learned LTM (D = 3.8667): Δ=19.0%, Cohen's d=-0.2835. Prior knowledge outperforms real-time learning at 2400-frame trial length — D needs more frames to build competitive patterns.

STM-only (B = 3.6) vs no memory (A = 3.3333): Δ=8.0%. STM recording alone does not change action selection (expected — STM feeds consolidation, not direct recall). Any difference reflects stochasticity, not learning.
Action entropy — A: 0.538, D: 0.541 (Δ=0.004). Conditions produce similar action diversity.
Condition D created 7.8 patterns on average with mean reliability 5.8%. Low pattern reliability — Laplace smoothing initialises at 0.5 but patterns may not be strengthened enough.
Average consolidation events per trial in D: 806.9. Active consolidation pipeline — STM events are frequently triggering LTM writes.
recommendations: LTM is almost unused (0.0%). Reduce ltmConfidenceThreshold from 0.25 to 0.15, or extend trial duration to ≥2400 frames so patterns can accumulate enough usageCount to build confidence.

Pattern reliability is low (5.8%). The success threshold in strengthenPattern() uses rewardThreshold * 0.5 = 7.5. Consider lowering to rewardThreshold * 0.3, or increasing agent reward density (more food items).

Pre-seeded knowledge (C) outperforms real-time learning (D) by 15.9%. 2400-frame trials are too short for D to build competitive LTM. Run Experiment 2 with trialDuration = 3600 frames to test whether D eventually surpasses C.
Run per-personality analysis: inspect conditions[cond].agents[0/1/2] in the summary JSON to determine whether AGGRESSIVE, CURIOUS, or CAUTIOUS agents benefit most from dual memory.

Run a paired t-test on allFoodEaten arrays in conditions.D vs conditions.A (n=15 per condition). The rawTrials array in the summary JSON contains all agent-level observations for import into Python/R.
